content.problemStatement:
Chef bought some number of sacks of flour, and some number of sacks of sugar. It is unknown exactly how many of each.

Each sack of flour weighs 222 kilograms, and each sack of sugar weighs 111 kilogram. It is known that the total weight of Chef's sacks of flours and sugar was NNN kilograms.

Is it possible that Chef bought an equal number of sacks of flour and sugar? Print Yes\text{Yes}Yes if it is possible and No\text{No}No otherwise.


Input Format

The first and only line contains 111 integer - NNN.


Output Format
Output Yes\text{Yes}Yes if it was possible for Chef to buy an equal number of sacks of rice and sugar, and No\text{No}No otherwise.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Chef could have bought 111 sack of flour and 111 sack of sugar for a total weight of 333 kilograms.

Explanation:
It is impossible for Chef to buy an equal amount of sacks of flour and sugar.
